arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i -E 'binutils|llvm' | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using the BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
LIBNAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try the ELF debug info format
curl -sfL -o /tmp/symbols/_.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "Debug symbols download failed"

# Also try the library itself with symbols
curl -sfL -o /tmp/symbols/${LIBNAME} \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIBNAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${LIBNAME}" && echo "Downloaded library" || echo "Library download failed"
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at offsets:

- #01 pc 0x222098
- #02 pc 0x2241ac
- #03 pc 0x1abc80
- #04 pc 0x1abd14
- #05 pc 0x16dfa4
- #06 pc 0x16db6c


- #07 pc 0x16de04

To resolve these addresses, I'll need to locate the debug symbols for this specific build using the BuildId, then apply `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to map each offset to its corresponding function and source location. Microsoft publishes .NET runtime symbols that should be available through a symbol server. I'm going to try a few different approaches to locate the symbols, starting with checking what debugging tools I have available on the system.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; apt list --installed 2>/dev/null | grep -i -E 'binutils|llvm' | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using the BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
LIBNAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try the ELF debug info format
curl -sfL -o /tmp/symbols/_.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" && echo "Downloaded debug symbols" || echo "Debug symbols download failed"

# Also try the library itself with symbols
curl -sfL -o /tmp/symbols/${LIBNAME} \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${LIBNAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${LIBNAME}" && echo "Downloaded library" || echo "Library download failed"
arguments.description: Download debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server

arguments.command:
# Symbolicate all the libmonosgen frames using the debug symbols
# Use llvm-addr2line which handles cross-architecture ELF files
DBGFILE="/tmp/symbols/_.debug"

for addr in 0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04; do
  echo -n "  $addr => "
  llvm-addr2line -f -e "$DBGFILE" "$addr" | paste - - | sed 's/\t/ at /'
done

data.content:
Here's the symbolicated backtrace for the `libmonosgen-2.0.so` frames:

```
#00 pc 0x6ae1c   libc.so                (abort+164)
#01 pc 0x222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast          at icall.c:6244
#02 pc 0x2241ac  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw      at icall-def.h:202
#03 pc 0x1abc80  libmonosgen-2.0.so     do_icall                                       at interp/interp.c:2457
#04 pc 0x1abd14  libmonosgen-2.0.so     get_interp_to_native_trampoline                at interp/interp.c:1732
#05 pc 0x16dfa4  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  at type-checking.c:0
#06 pc 0x16db6c  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  at type-checking.c:506
#07 pc 0x16de04  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                  at type-checking.c:528
#08 pc 0x307c    <anonymous>            (JIT/AOT managed code)
```

**Summary:** The crash is a `SIGABRT` triggered by `Environment.FailFast("not combatible")` called from managed code. The native call path goes through the Mono interpreter (`do_icall` → `get_interp_to_native_trampoline`) after a type-checking operation (`handle_isinst`). Frame `#08` is JIT-compiled managed code, which would need the app's managed symbols to resolve further.
